title: Stenia beaumontii
taxonomy: Stenia beaumontii is classified as a species of orchid. It is a type of terrestrial orchid belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The genus Stenia is part of the subtribe Cynorkidinae. Within the Apocynaceae family, orchids are actually in the family Apocynaceae but belong to the subfamily Apocynoideae. The Cynorkidinae subtribe is part of the Apocynoideae subfamily.
uses: Stenia beaumontii is a plant species that is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the southeastern United States. Its medicinal uses include treating various ailments such as fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. intro: Stenia beaumontii, also known as Beaumont's Stenia or Beaumont's Desert Marigold, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Stenia beaumontii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory conditions and promote overall well-being.
